content: arXiv 2603.00325v1 Announce Type  new Abstract  This paper introduces inspection through GLASS, a Geometric Look-Angle Shaping Strategy for enclosed regions using unmanned aerial vehicles. In doing so, the vehicles guidance command is constructed through a bounded, geometry-consistent shaping of the look angle relative to a desired standoff path. By embedding a smooth, hyperbolic-tangent-type shaping function within a polar geometric framework, GLASS ensures global existence of the guidance dynamics. It avoids the far-field limitations inherent to conventional formulations. Lyapunov stability analysis establishes asymptotic convergence to a prescribed inspection standoff under explicit curvature feasibility conditions, along with analytical settling-time characteristics. The proposed strategy incorporates maximum turn-rate constraints without inducing singularities throughout the workspace. High-fidelity six-degree-of-freedom quadrotor simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of GLASS in representative enclosed inspection scenarios, highlighting a practically viable guidance framework for autonomous enclosed inspection missions.

metadata.author: Amit Shivam, Manuel C. R. M. Fernandes, Sergio Vinha, Fernando A. C. C. Fontes
metadata.summary: 00325v1 Announce Type  new Abstract  This paper introduces inspection through GLASS, a Geometric Look-Angle Shaping Strategy for enclosed regions using unmanned aerial vehicles
